element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation for all combat; damage output differs significantly between Japanese (higher) and International versions
Limited throw uses in Japanese version (1 use per pickup); International version allows 6 uses per pickup for sustained offensive pressure
Same use limitation as spear; ranged alternative to melee
Requires power bar buildup; Japanese version drains player health (5 HP weak, 20 HP strong); International version removes health cost, making it more accessible
High-damage finishing move; costs 40 HP in Japanese version, 12 HP in International; full power bar charges slower in Japanese version (30 frames longer)
Defensive/evasion tool; 44 seconds duration in Japanese version, reduced to 17 seconds in International

element_introduction_order.element: Charger Alien enemy (two-player vs mode only)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Opponent player control; removed entirely in International versions
Thematic shift from urban outdoor area (Japan) to waterfall basin (International); suggests environmental navigation challenges

typical_relief_triggers: Picking up spear or disc weapons
Building power bar for special attacks
typical_tension_triggers: Low health states (International version starts higher)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based combat rhythm with resource management via limited weapon uses (Japanese version) and health-draining specials (Japanese version); International version increases accessibility through higher starting HP, longer weapon use counts, and non-draining specials

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Level select accessible via hidden button combination (A+X+L+R on Controller 2, Start on Controller 1 from options menu)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Novice and Standard difficulties available; Standard gives 80 HP (JP) / 96 HP (INT); basic melee combat foundational
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of weapon pickups and special attacks; power bar management becomes critical; Japanese version power bar slower to charge
difficulty_curve.late_game: Full laser strike and advanced enemy patterns; Advanced (64 HP) and Expert (48 HP) difficulties equivalent across regions
notable_spikes: Stage 3 transition introduces environmental hazard change (urban to waterfall)
Stages 8-10 unknown from source but implied late-game intensity

notable_level_patterns.description: Stage 3 completely redesigned between Japanese (urban outdoor) and International (waterfall basin) versions; suggests deliberate level-specific localization or narrative context changes
Fixed sequence of 10 stages with password access (unused in release) and hidden level select for development/testing

sources_used: tcrf - Alien vs. Predator (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
